cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking how long the experiment lasted according to the text. The options are (A) about three days, (B) about ten days, (C) about two weeks, or (D) about a month. First, I need to go back to the text and look for any information about the duration of the experiment. Let me scan through the passage again. The text mentions that the participants were assigned to write for 20 minutes per day for three consecutive days. The first part of the experiment, the writing phase, lasted three days. Then, it says, "Two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a standard 4mm skin biopsy..." So after the initial three days of writing, there was a two-week waiting period before the biopsy was conducted. Wait, but the question is about how long the entire experiment lasted. Let me check further. The biopsy was done two weeks after the writing sessions. Then, the wounds were photographed regularly over the following days to determine the healing rate. The results were observed up to the 11th day after the biopsy. So the timeline is: three days of writing, then two weeks until the biopsy, and then 11 more days until the wounds were fully healed in some participants. But the main experiment's duration is likely from the start of the writing sessions until the end of the wound healing observation. So three days of writing, two weeks waiting until the biopsy, and then 11 days of observation. That adds up to about three days + 14 days
